name: Old Chinese
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Old Chinese (c. 1250 BCE-220 CE) is the oldest attested stage of Sinitic and the ancestor of Middle Chinese. It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Baxter, W. H. & Sagart, L.
sources.title: Old Chinese: A New Reconstruction
sources.notes: Reference for Old Chinese reconstruction (ancestry node only).
